campbell.title: Campbell City Council — February 3, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to approve a $4,000 donation from Water Tower Kitchen to help pay for youth recreation scholarships. They also discussed plans to build a bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 at Hamilton Avenue, including reviewing a feasibility study and applying for grant funding to help pay for the project.
keyTopics: Water Tower Kitchen donates $4,000 for youth recreation scholarships
Hamilton Avenue bicycle and pedestrian bridge over Highway 17 project moving forward
City applying for state grants to fund the Hamilton Avenue bridge
Extension of interim ordinance 2326 under consideration

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 4, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council decided which community groups and events would receive grant money from the city for the upcoming fiscal year. They approved specific dollar amounts for different organizations and also clarified the rules for how community groups can apply for event funding and street closure permits.
keyTopics: Approved which organizations get city grant money and how much each receives
Set aside funding for the Competitive Event Grant Program for the fiscal year
Updated the rules for Community Event and Street Closure Grants to make them clearer

san-jose.title: San José City Council — March 25, 2026
san-jose.summary: This San José City Council meeting on March 25, 2026 provided information about how residents can watch the meeting through multiple channels including cable TV, online streaming, and phone call-in options. Live translations in over 50 languages were available to help non-English speakers follow along.
keyTopics: Multiple ways to watch the meeting (cable, YouTube, Zoom, phone)
Live translations available in 50+ languages
Meeting access information for residents
No public comment period during this session

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — March 25, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council's zoning hearing that was supposed to happen on March 25, 2026 has been canceled. The next zoning hearing will take place on April 8, 2026 instead. If you had plans to attend or present anything about zoning issues, mark your calendar for the new date.
keyTopics: March 25 zoning hearing cancelled
Rescheduled to April 8, 2026
Administrative zoning decisions postponed

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — March 25, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review and approve funding applications for community development grants that help pay for local housing and support services. The commission also approved meeting notes from their previous February gathering.
keyTopics: Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding applications for housing projects
Review of funding applicants seeking grants for human services programs
Approval of February 25, 2026 meeting minutes

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — March 27, 2026
cupertino.summary: The city council focused on helping local businesses grow and succeed. They discussed a program to help businesses get their licenses in order, ways to encourage people to shop at local stores and shopping centers, and financial incentives to attract new retail shops and startups to Cupertino.
keyTopics: Business License Amnesty Program - helping businesses catch up on licenses without penalties
Shopping center outreach - connecting with local malls and commercial areas
Summer Shop Local Campaign - promoting Cupertino businesses to residents
City event sponsorships - partnering with private companies to fund community events
Economic incentives - offering deals to attract new retail and startup businesses

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — March 24, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on March 24, 2026 to address several administrative matters and get public input on two main issues: a weed abatement program to address overgrown weeds around the city, and a plan for how federal housing grant money will be spent in the next fiscal year. The council also handled various postponements and exceptions to regular procedures.
keyTopics: Weed abatement program - city working to remove overgrown weeds on properties
Federal housing grant spending plan for 2026-27 - public input being collected
Multiple agenda items delayed or reconsidered for further discussion

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 23, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met on March 23, 2026 to recognize Women's History Month and approve two agreements. The council agreed to let the American Red Cross use city facilities during emergencies, and approved a $93,383 contract with a company called Insight Public Sector to provide email security and maintenance services for the city for three years.
keyTopics: Women's History Month recognition proclamation
Emergency disaster facility agreement with American Red Cross
Email security software contract ($93,383 for 3 years)
